frameworks.name: sensory-biased working memory
frameworks.one_line: frontal subregions show modality-preferential recruitment during WM

frameworks.name: supramodal working memory
frameworks.one_line: regions recruited by WM across all sensory modalities

frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: modality-selective regions segregate into distinct intrinsic networks

core_question: How are visual-, auditory-, tactile-selective and supramodal working memory regions jointly organized in individual human frontal cortex?
key_assumptions: modality selectivity persists beyond primary sensory cortices
individual-subject mapping reveals joint topography missed by group averages
manipulations: sensory modality of N-back WM (auditory vs visual vs tactile)
blocked fMRI task design within subject
key_findings: confirmed multiple visual- and auditory-biased frontal regions
identified bilateral tactile-selective regions in dorsal/ventral precentral sulcus, postcentral sulcus, anterior IPS
supramodal WM regions overlap heavily with visual-biased frontal and parietal regions
modality-selective WM regions segregate into modality-specific resting-state networks
interpretations: frontal cortex contains a fine-grained mosaic of modality-biased and supramodal WM subregions
visual-biased and supramodal recruitment share substrate more than tactile or auditory do
